contract: research sources, competitor notes, keyword/entity findings, source dates, evidence gaps

inputsUsed: discovery/phase-0-discovery.v2.json (Phase-1 enrichment: owner-since-2006, catfish signature, cornmeal/lemon-pepper breading, press menu additions)

researchSources.what: SugaPeach Chicken & Fish Fry — primary direct competitor, own web presence
Iowa Soul Food Company brand umbrella (SugaPeach)
Harold's Chicken Cedar Rapids reopening (SW side)
SoulGood Southern Diner (3100 6th St SW) profile
Chicago Grill Chicken & Fish — chicken/fish competitor
Primary: owner background, signature dishes, menu specifics
1616 property facts: 1,895 sqft, large dining room, retail counter, onsite parking, high-visibility 1st Ave NE

sourceDates.source: The Gazette — Harold's Chicken CR reopening

competitorNotes.name: SugaPeach Chicken & Fish Fry (Iowa Soul Food Company)
competitorNotes.positioning: Primary direct competitor — fried chicken + fish + soul sides (mac & cheese praised). Part of a multi-brand Iowa Soul Food Company; has its own website + brand identity.
competitorNotes.threat: Owned web presence and brand umbrella = stronger existing search footprint than Captains (who has none).
competitorNotes.gapCaptainsExploits: Captains' owner-since-2006 multi-state track record + catfish signature + made-to-order angle; a real marketing site closes SugaPeach's web-presence advantage.

competitorNotes.name: Harold's Chicken
competitorNotes.positioning: Recognized Chicago fried-chicken brand; reopened in Cedar Rapids (SW side, near Wawa Caribbean, former Highway 30 Diner) in Feb 2026.
competitorNotes.threat: National brand recognition for fried chicken.
competitorNotes.gapCaptainsExploits: Captains = fish AND chicken (broader), NE-side location (1st Ave corridor) vs Harold's SW side — different trade area.

competitorNotes.name: SoulGood Southern Diner
competitorNotes.positioning: African-American + Asian soul cuisine, fried chicken/fish; listed at 3100 6th St SW (Gazette 2021).
competitorNotes.threat: Established soul-food identity with press history.
competitorNotes.gapCaptainsExploits: Captains' fried-fish specialization + NE trade area.
competitorNotes.note: 3100 6th St SW is ALSO Captains' out-of-scope 2nd-location address — ambiguous (prior tenant / co-address / adjacent unit). Flagged in evidenceGaps.

competitorNotes.name: Chicago Grill Chicken & Fish
competitorNotes.threat: Overlapping menu category on delivery apps.
competitorNotes.gapCaptainsExploits: Direct brand site + made-to-order + local-owner story.

competitorNotes.name: Yelp long-tail (unverified individually)
competitorNotes.note: Named in Yelp soul-food results; open/closed status, catering-only status, and menu overlap NOT individually verified — see evidenceGaps.

keywordEntityFindings.volumeCaveat: No keyword tool available (Tier-1 skill, no Ahrefs/SEMrush integration). Clusters below are derived from live SERP signals + intent reasoning, NOT tool-verified search volumes. Volumes are directional only — see evidenceGaps.

keywords: captains fish and chicken cedar rapids

geoAiCitationAngles: Answer-unit: 'What is Captains Fish and Chicken known for?' -> catfish (most popular), made-to-order, cornmeal + fresh lemon-pepper breading
Answer-unit: 'fried fish + chicken on the NE side of Cedar Rapids' -> 1st Ave NE / Mound View, near Hy-Vee, onsite parking
Owner-authority answer-unit: Ray Aqel, restaurateur since 2006, first Iowa location

localKnowledgeRecord.corridor: 1st Ave NE (high-visibility arterial NE of downtown Cedar Rapids)
localKnowledgeRecord.nearbyLandmark: Hy-Vee (cited as adjacent landmark for prior tenant at 1616)
localKnowledgeRecord.premises: ~1,895 sqft; kitchen + retail counter + large dining room + 2 restrooms
localKnowledgeRecord.dineInCorrection: Space HAS a large dining room -> dine-in seating exists; owner still says most orders go out (takeout-dominant). Corrects Phase-0 'limited/unconfirmed'.
localKnowledgeRecord.foldNote: This is a single fixed-location restaurant, NOT a multi-city service-area business. Distance/drive-time matrix tables (founder P0 city-record) are largely N/A; neighborhood + occasion + corridor facts are the anti-doorway payload instead.
localKnowledgeRecord.unfabricated: No invented highways/landmarks/drive-times. Exact cross-street and lat/lng not captured (evidence gap).

checklist.item: City/location knowledge record (distance, drive-time, route, landmarks, vibe)
checklist.reason: Single fixed-location restaurant; captured Mound View + 1st Ave NE corridor + Hy-Vee + parking + dining room. Distance/drive-time matrix N/A (no multi-city service area); lat/lng + exact cross-street not captured.

checklist.item: Trigger-event/occasion page taxonomy keyed to customer moment
checklist.reason: Identified occasions: quick lunch, family dinner takeout, Friday fish fry, game-day wings, late-night. Feeds Phase 2 restaurant page families.
Owner confirmed NO catering — do not create a catering occasion/page.

checklist.item: Rich business-entity research (servesCuisine, knowsAbout[], hours, menu, priceRange)
checklist.reason: Cuisine + knowsAbout strings + hours + menu captured. priceRange unknown (owner disclaimed delivery-app prices).
No owner-authoritative prices; delivery-app prices disclaimed. Cannot build price-range answer units until provided.

Review volume confirmed thin (Yelp 1 review; FB 17 recommends; no located Google rating). NO aggregateRating schema to emit; no fabricated reviews. Even '100% 5-star' reframing is too thin at 1 review.
Named owner Ray Aqel, restaurateur since 2006, multi-state track record; press citations (Gazette, KHAK) available for author/authority surface.
SugaPeach (primary), Harold's Chicken, SoulGood, Chicago Grill + Yelp long-tail.

checklist.item: GBP claim status + NAP citation consistency
checklist.reason: NAP consistent across Yelp/Uber Eats/DoorDash/Grubhub/Postmates, but Google Business Profile claim status + Google rating NOT located after 3 attempts. Hand to launch-phase verification.

checklist.item: Deploy-blocking validation gate / matrix mesh / two-tier llms.txt / perf-a11y-security bundle
checklist.reason: These are engine/build patterns (founder P0-P1), not Phase-1 research deliverables. Carried to Phase 2 (plan) and Phase 4 (build). llms.txt treated as low-priority hygiene per the catalog's June-2026 GEO caveat.

evidenceGaps: Keyword search VOLUMES are not tool-verified (no keyword tool); clusters are SERP+intent reasoned, directional only.
Google Business Profile claim status and Google star rating/review count could NOT be located via web search (3 attempts).

Competitor long-tail (I'm Soul Hungry, Pee Wees, Royceann's, Black Sheep Social Club, Pick Up N Go, Rodney's Kitchen, Shugga's) not individually verified for open/closed, catering-only, or menu overlap.
SoulGood Southern Diner at 3100 6th St SW vs Captains' out-of-scope 2nd location at the same address — relationship ambiguous (prior tenant / adjacent unit / co-address).
No owner-authoritative menu prices -> no priceRange / price answer units.
Exact cross-street and lat/lng for 1616 not captured; neighborhood-level only.
Press-sourced menu additions (liver, mashed potatoes, mac & cheese) and seasoning/signature claims still pending owner confirmation (carried from Phase 0 v2).
Nearby-town pull (Marion, Hiawatha) assumed as geo-modifiers, not confirmed by owner as actual customer draw.
handoffToPhase2: Restaurant page-matrix families: menu-category pages + occasion pages (quick lunch / family dinner / Friday fish fry / game-day wings / late-night) + intent pages — NOT service x location (single location).
Anti-doorway payload = neighborhood + corridor + occasion facts (no fabricated drive-time matrix).
Conversion surface constraints from Phase 0 still bind: no lead-form endpoint (form can't ship), call-only CTA, trust grid omitted, no prices.
Competitor differentiation angle: owner-since-2006 + catfish signature + made-to-order + real brand site vs SugaPeach/Harold's.
